evals.prompt: I want to build a MatrixScan Batch web app from scratch to simultaneously track multiple barcodes. I don't have any existing code yet.
evals.expected_output: The skill offers the pre-built sample as the starting point, provides the MatrixScanSimpleSample URL, and guides the user through cloning it, replacing the license key, and adjusting symbologies.

assertions.text: The pre-built sample is offered before any manual integration code is shown
The MatrixScanSimpleSample URL (datacapture-web-samples/.../MatrixScanSimpleSample) is mentioned
Replacing the license key placeholder is mentioned
Adjusting symbologies is mentioned
Cross-origin headers (COOP/COEP or multithreading) are mentioned as a requirement

evals.prompt: I want to add MatrixScan Batch to my web app to track multiple barcodes simultaneously. Here's my main file: empty-app.ts. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, writes complete BarcodeBatch* integration code into empty-app.ts, mentions COOP/COEP header requirement, and shows the setup checklist.

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown (mentions @scandit/web-datacapture-core and @scandit/web-datacapture-barcode)
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is present with libraryLocation and moduleLoaders
barcodeCaptureLoader() is present in moduleLoaders
License key placeholder (e.g. ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY or similar) is present
BarcodeBatch.forContext( is present (async factory pattern, not new BarcodeBatch())
await is used before BarcodeBatch.forContext(
BarcodeBatchSettings is created with new BarcodeBatchSettings()
enableSymbologies is called on settings
Symbology.EAN13UPCA is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
BarcodeBatch.recommendedCameraSettings is referenced as a static property (not a method call)
switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.On) is present
BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay.withBarcodeBatchForView or withBarcodeBatchForViewWithStyle is awaited
Cross-origin isolation headers (COOP/COEP or multithreading requirement) are mentioned
Import from '@scandit/web-datacapture-core' is present
Import from '@scandit/web-datacapture-barcode' is present

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Batch web app I need to react to tracked barcodes each frame. Show me how to receive addedTrackedBarcodes, updatedTrackedBarcodes, and removedTrackedBarcodes, and iterate all currently tracked barcodes. File: empty-app.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill implements a BarcodeBatchListener with didUpdateSession, accesses session.addedTrackedBarcodes, session.updatedTrackedBarcodes, session.removedTrackedBarcodes (noting these are strings), and iterates Object.values(session.trackedBarcodes).

assertions.text: didUpdateSession is implemented in the BarcodeBatchListener
session.addedTrackedBarcodes is accessed (semantic)
session.updatedTrackedBarcodes is accessed (semantic)
session.removedTrackedBarcodes is accessed (semantic)
session.trackedBarcodes is accessed and iterated (semantic)
removedTrackedBarcodes identifiers are noted or treated as strings (semantic — e.g. parseInt or string type annotation)
barcodeBatch.addListener( is called with the listener
BarcodeBatch.forContext( is present
'-- ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY HERE --' is present

evals.prompt: I want to color the barcode highlights differently: EAN-13 barcodes in green, Code 128 in blue, and hide everything else. Use brushForTrackedBarcode. File: integrated-app.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets overlay.listener with a brushForTrackedBarcode callback that returns a green Brush for EAN13UPCA, a blue Brush for Code128, and null (or transparent Brush) for everything else.

assertions.text: brushForTrackedBarcode callback is implemented on overlay.listener
A green Brush is returned for Symbology.EAN13UPCA (semantic)
A blue Brush is returned for Symbology.Code128 (semantic)
null or a transparent Brush is returned for other symbologies (semantic)
Brush is imported from @scandit/web-datacapture-core
BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay is present (withBarcodeBatchForView or withBarcodeBatchForViewWithStyle)

evals.prompt: I want to add custom AR bubbles above each tracked barcode in my MatrixScan Batch web app. Each bubble should show the barcode's data value. File: integrated-app.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill implements BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay with TrackedBarcodeView.withHTMLElement to attach custom HTML elements to each tracked barcode, using anchorForTrackedBarcode and offsetForTrackedBarcode for positioning.

assertions.text: BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay.withBarcodeBatchForView is awaited
TrackedBarcodeView.withHTMLElement is used (not a subclass)
A DOM element (e.g. document.createElement) is created and populated with barcode data (semantic)
anchorForTrackedBarcode or setAnchorForTrackedBarcode is present
offsetForTrackedBarcode or setOffsetForTrackedBarcode is present
TrackedBarcodeView is imported from @scandit/web-datacapture-barcode

evals.prompt: I already have a DataCaptureContext and a configured BarcodeBatch in my web app. Now I need to build the camera preview surface: create the DataCaptureView from the context, attach it to my <div id="data-capture-view">, and add a BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay to it. File: empty-app.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill builds the DataCaptureView with DataCaptureView.forContext(context), mounts it with view.connectToElement(document.getElementById('data-capture-view')), creates a BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay, and attaches it to the view (either by passing the view to the overlay factory, or by creating the overlay with a null view and calling await view.addOverlay(overlay)).

assertions.text: DataCaptureView.forContext( is awaited to build the view
view.connectToElement( is called with the DOM element (e.g. document.getElementById('data-capture-view'))
A BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay is created (withBarcodeBatchForView or withBarcodeBatchForViewWithStyle)
The overlay is attached to the view — either the view is passed to the overlay factory, or view.addOverlay( is awaited (semantic)
DataCaptureView is imported from '@scandit/web-datacapture-core'

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Batch web app I want the tracked-barcode highlights to render as dots instead of frames. File: empty-app.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill creates the BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay using withBarcodeBatchForViewWithStyle and passes BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayStyle.Dot as the style argument, noting that .Frame is the alternative.

assertions.text: BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay.withBarcodeBatchForViewWithStyle is awaited
BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayStyle.Dot is passed as the style argument
BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayStyle.Frame is mentioned as the alternative style (semantic)
BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayStyle is imported from '@scandit/web-datacapture-barcode'

evals.prompt: When the user taps one of the highlighted tracked barcodes in my MatrixScan Batch web app, I want to log its data value. I'm using the BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay. File: integrated-app.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets overlay.listener with a didTapTrackedBarcode callback that reads trackedBarcode.barcode.data, on the BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay.

assertions.text: didTapTrackedBarcode callback is implemented on the BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay listener
trackedBarcode.barcode.data is read inside the callback (semantic)
The listener is assigned to the BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay (overlay.listener =) (semantic)

evals.prompt: I have custom AR bubbles over each tracked barcode using BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay. When the user taps one of the AR bubbles, I want to open a detail panel for that barcode. File: integrated-app.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a didTapViewForTrackedBarcode callback to the BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay listener that reacts to the tapped trackedBarcode (e.g. reads trackedBarcode.barcode.data).

assertions.text: didTapViewForTrackedBarcode callback is implemented on the BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay listener
The callback reacts to the tapped trackedBarcode (e.g. reads trackedBarcode.barcode.data) (semantic)
BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay is used (withBarcodeBatchForView)

evals.prompt: I want my MatrixScan Batch web app to beep when a new barcode starts being tracked. There's no beep happening right now. File: empty-app.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill explains that BarcodeBatch has no automatic feedback (no barcodeBatch.feedback property) and emits a Feedback manually: it builds a Feedback (e.g. Feedback.defaultFeedback) once and calls feedback.emit() inside didUpdateSession when session.addedTrackedBarcodes is non-empty.

assertions.text: It is stated that BarcodeBatch has no automatic/built-in feedback and the beep must be emitted manually (semantic)
A Feedback is obtained — Feedback.defaultFeedback or new Feedback( (semantic)
feedback.emit() is called to play the beep
emit() is called from didUpdateSession when session.addedTrackedBarcodes has new barcodes (semantic)
Feedback is imported from '@scandit/web-datacapture-core'
The Feedback instance is created once and reused, not recreated every frame (semantic)
